content:
---
title: "AutoProxy makes four decisions per message: answer, route, absorb, or escalate"
description: "Every message is classified and then actioned into one of four dispositions that replace human triage"
date: 2026-03-14
group: zk
type: note
id: "20260314-four-decisions-per-message"
links:
  - "20260314-faster-inbox-vs-smaller-inbox"
  - "20260314-deterministic-rules-before-llm"
  - "20260314-auto-response-allowlist-structured-data-only"
  - "20260314-asymmetric-risk-false-low-worse-than-false-high"
source: "projects/autoproxy/PRD"
created_by: agent
---

AutoProxy's core function reduces to four possible decisions for every message that passes through it.

**1. Answer it.** The question has a factual answer in the system. AutoProxy responds immediately with data from the PMS or knowledge base. Example: a housekeeper asks "Is room 412 a late checkout?" and gets the checkout time from the reservation system within 5 seconds. This is the highest-value action because it eliminates the interruption entirely -- neither the sender nor any human recipient spends time on it.

**2. Route it.** The question needs a human, but not the one who was asked. AutoProxy sends it to the right person based on intent classification, role matching, and schedule awareness. Example: a maintenance request in the general channel is routed to the on-duty maintenance tech, bypassing the front desk agent who would normally act as relay.

**3. Absorb it.** The message is informational -- a status update, FYI, or acknowledgment. AutoProxy files it, acknowledges receipt if appropriate, and surfaces it in the next batch delivery or digest. The recipient sees it when they have time, not when it was sent.

**4. Escalate it.** The message is urgent or exceptional. AutoProxy bypasses normal routing and alerts the right person immediately, with full context pre-assembled. Escalation walks up the chain: department member to lead to GM to regional to executive, with timeouts at each level.

The design principle underlying these four decisions: AutoProxy should make every person feel like they have a dedicated chief of staff who handles triage, answers routine questions, and only interrupts them for things that genuinely need their attention. The four decisions map to how a skilled executive assistant would handle a manager's inbox.
